results.title: Iraq’s oil minister says US energy deals worth about $200 billion
results.description: Iraq's oil minister said on Tuesday that agreements ​signed between the oil ministry ‌and U.S. companies during Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi's visit to ​the U.S. were estimated at $200 ​billion.

results.title: Record platform inflows put ASX wealth names in focus
results.description: HUB24, ASX Limited, Pinnacle and Challenger draw focus as record platform inflows lift the diversified financials corner of the ASX.

results.title: Drivers get a break: I-285 weekend closure called off due to weather
results.description: The work is part of the I-285 Westside Rebuild project, a $155 million effort to reconstruct and rehabilitate concrete pavement.

results.title: To ease storage crunch, Punjab demands 12 LMT monthly foodgrain lifting
results.description: A target of procuring 180 LMT of paddy for the central pool has been demanded from the Centre in respect of the upcoming paddy procurement season

results.title: How Flexibility Markets Are Transforming Europe’s Power Sector
results.description: Europe’s transition toward a low-carbon energy system is accelerating, driven by ambitious climate policies and rapid renewable energy deployment. However, as wind and solar generation increase, maintaining a stable and reliable electricity supply has become a pressing challenge. This is where the Europe Adequacy & Flexibility (Capacity + BESS) Market plays a critical role in supporting grid resilience and ensuring energy security. The variability of renewable energy sources introduces...

results.title: China's Zhongji Innolight seeks $55 billion Hong Kong listing, Asia's No. 2 in 2026
results.description: Chinese optical parts maker Zhongji Innolight aims to raise up to HK$55.05 billion in a Hong Kong listing, in what would be Asia's second-largest share sale this year, an exchange filing showed on Wednesday.

results.title: Yen slides past 163 to fresh four-decade low, raising intervention alert
results.description: The yen was pinned near an almost four-decade low on Wednesday as rising oil prices and US Treasury yields hoisted the dollar and left traders on edge about possible Japanese intervention.
